title: Human Development Index groups
subtitle: The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. The groups below define different levels of human development depending on the HDI values.
caption:
Human Development Index groups, 2023

The Human Development Index (HDI) is a summary measure of key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, a good education, and a decent standard of living. The groups below define different levels of human development depending on the HDI values.

This choropleth map displays the 2023 Human Development Index groups for countries worldwide, using a four-color scale to categorize nations into Low, Medium, High, and Very High development levels. The visualization reveals a distinct geographic distribution of these categories. The Very High group, represented by dark blue, encompasses most of North America, including the United States and Canada, the majority of Western and Northern Europe, Australia, New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, and several nations in South America like Chile and Argentina. The High group, shown in light blue, covers a broad swath including much of Eastern Europe, Russia, China, Brazil, Mexico, and parts of the Middle East such as Saudi Arabia. The Medium group, colored yellow, is prevalent across much of South Asia, including India, Southeast Asia, parts of North Africa like Egypt, and several countries in Latin America. The Low group, marked in red, is concentrated primarily in sub-Saharan Africa, including nations like Niger, Chad, the Central African Republic, and Somalia, with Yemen also falling into this category. Greenland is shown with diagonal hatching, indicating no data is available. The map shows a clear pattern where the highest development levels are clustered in the Americas, Europe, and parts of East Asia and Oceania, while the lowest levels are predominantly found in a large contiguous area of Africa and a few countries in Asia.
